name: British foot [1865]
longName: Uses Clark's estimate of 1853-1865 British foot-metre ratio of 0.9144025 metres per yard. Used in 1962 and 1975 estimates of Indian foot.
aliasNames: Uses Clark's estimate of 1853-1865 British foot-metre ratio of 0.9144025 metres per yard. Used in 1962 and 1975 estimates of Indian foot.

name: British foot [Benoit 1895 A]
longName: Uses Benoit's 1895 British yard-metre ratio as given by Clark as 0.9143992 metre.s per yard. Used for deriving metric size of ellipsoid in Palestine.
aliasNames: Uses Benoit's 1895 British yard-metre ratio as given by Clark as 0.9143992 metre.s per yard. Used for deriving metric size of ellipsoid in Palestine.

name: British foot [Benoit 1895 B]
longName: Uses Benoit's 1895 British yard-metre ratio as given by Bomford as 39.370113 inches per metre. Used in West Malaysian mapping.
aliasNames: Uses Benoit's 1895 British yard-metre ratio as given by Bomford as 39.370113 inches per metre. Used in West Malaysian mapping.

name: British foot [Sears 1922]
longName: Uses Sear's 1922 British yard-metre ratio as given by Bomford as 39.370147 inches per metre. Used in East Malaysian and older New Zealand mapping.
aliasNames: Uses Sear's 1922 British yard-metre ratio as given by Bomford as 39.370147 inches per metre. Used in East Malaysian and older New Zealand mapping.

name: Clarke foot
longName: Assumes Clarke's 1865 ratio of 1 British foot = 0.3047972654 French legal metres applies to the international metre.
aliasNames: Assumes Clarke's 1865 ratio of 1 British foot = 0.3047972654 French legal metres applies to the international metre.

name: Gold Coast foot

aliasNames: Used in Ghana and some adjacent parts of British west Africa prior to metrication, except for the metrication of projection defining parameters when British foot (Sears 1922) used.

name: US survey chain

name: chain

name: delta Rankine
longName: Change in degrees Rankine. Rankine = (Celsius + 273.15) x 9/5 so a difference in Fahrenheit temperature cancels out the 273.15 offset. For temperature intervals rather than specific temperatures, 1 deltaF = 1 deltaR = 5/9 deltaC = 5/9 deltaK.

name: delta kelvin
longName: Change in kelvin. A zero difference in Kelvin temperature is not the same as being at zero on the Kelvin temperature scale. The degree Celsius and the kelvin are equal in size, so that the numerical value of a temperature difference or temperature interval is the same when expressed in either degrees Celsius or in kelvins.

name: UK fluid-ounce
longName: United Kingdom 1/20 of UK pint. Therefore, 1/160 of UK gallon or (1/160)* 4.54609 cubic decimetres. From UK-1985.

longName: There are two pints to one quart

name: UK quart
longName: There are four quarts to one gallon.

name: US fluid-ounce
longName: 1/16 of US pint which is 1/8 of US gallon. Therefore, 1/128 of US gallon.
aliasNames: 1/16 of US pint which is 1/8 of US gallon. Therefore, 1/128 of US gallon.

name: US gallon
longName: The United States liquid gallon is legally defined as 231 cubic inches, and is equal to exactly 3.785411784 litres. Authorized tables, US Code, Title 15, chapter 6, subchapter I, section 205.
